---
title: "Microbial Strain Analysis — Exploratory Data Analysis"
author: "Will"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
exec(open('scripts/setup.py').read())
```

## Introduction

Comprehensive exploratory data analysis of a bacterial strain database provided as a
bioinformatics take-home challenge. The goal is to characterise taxonomic diversity,
growth parameters, and media/compound preferences of the strains.

**Objectives**

1. Quantify data completeness and flag quality issues.
2. Characterise taxonomic distribution across phyla and classes.
3. Understand growth parameter distributions (temperature, pH, O₂ tolerance).
4. Profile growth media — richness, strain coverage, and most common compounds.
5. Identify cross-variable patterns relevant to media prediction or strain clustering.

---

## Dataset Overview

The database consists of four relational CSV files:

| Table | Description |
|---|---|
| `strains.csv` | Taxonomic classification and growth parameters per strain |
| `strain_media.csv` | Maps each strain to its compatible growth media |
| `media_compounds.csv` | Maps each medium to its constituent compounds |
| `compounds.csv` | Compound ID–name lookup |

```{python}
#| label: dataset-overview
exec(open('scripts/s01_dataset_overview.py').read())
```

---

## Data Quality

```{python}
#| label: data-quality
exec(open('scripts/s02_data_quality.py').read())
```

::: {.callout-note}
Temperatures above 80°C or below 0°C may represent data entry errors (e.g., Fahrenheit
values not converted). pH values outside 0–14 should be flagged for validation.
:::

---

## Taxonomic Distribution

### Phylum-level Diversity

```{python}
#| label: taxonomy-phylum
exec(open('scripts/s03_taxonomy.py').read())
```

### Hierarchical View (Phylum → Class)

```{python}
#| label: taxonomy-treemap
exec(open('scripts/s03b_taxonomy_treemap.py').read())
```

### O₂ Tolerance & Phylum × O₂ Heatmap

```{python}
#| label: taxonomy-o2
exec(open('scripts/s03c_o2_phylum_heatmap.py').read())
```

::: {.callout-tip}
The heatmap highlights phyla that are exclusively aerobic, anaerobic, or facultative.
Firmicutes show strong anaerobic enrichment; Proteobacteria are predominantly aerobic.
:::

---

## Growth Parameters

### Temperature & pH Distributions

```{python}
#| label: growth-params
exec(open('scripts/s04_growth_params.py').read())
```

::: {.callout-note}
Mesophilic aerobes (~37°C, pH ~7) form the densest cluster. Thermophiles (>55°C)
largely occupy neutral-to-alkaline pH ranges.
:::

### Correlation Between Growth Parameters

```{python}
#| label: growth-correlation
exec(open('scripts/s04b_correlation.py').read())
```

---

## Media & Compounds

```{python}
#| label: media-compounds
exec(open('scripts/s05_media_compounds.py').read())
```

---

## Key Findings

```{python}
#| label: findings
exec(open('scripts/s06_findings.py').read())
```

---

## Further Exploration Directions

- **Media prediction**: classify preferred growth medium from growth parameters + taxonomy.
- **Strain clustering**: cluster strains by compound fingerprint using Jaccard / binary PCA.
- **Network analysis**: bipartite strain–compound network to identify compound communities.
- **Phylogenetic mapping**: overlay O₂ tolerance or temperature niche onto a taxonomic tree.
- **Outlier investigation**: extreme temp/pH values may be data errors or novel physiology.

---

## Session Info

```{python}
#| label: session-info
import importlib
for pkg in ["pandas", "numpy", "plotly", "scipy"]:
    print(f"{pkg}: {importlib.import_module(pkg).__version__}")
```
